Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1912 upvotes, 251 comments
  2. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 123 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. Standardizing source maps 62 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 364 upvotes, 63 comments
  5. Where did you think the training data was coming from? 29 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Faster Asin() Was Hiding in Plain Sight 51 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 81 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. The entities enabling scientific fraud at scale are large, resilient and growing 77 upvotes, 22 comments
  9. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 399 upvotes, 153 comments
  10. AI Agent Hacks McKinsey 105 upvotes, 33 comments
  11. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 157 upvotes, 57 comments
  12. Whistleblower: DOGE member took Social Security data to new job 278 upvotes, 111 comments
  13. SSH Secret Menu 291 upvotes, 129 comments
  14. PeppyOS: A simpler alternative to ROS 2 (now with containers support) 44 upvotes, 14 comments
  15. Levels of Agentic Engineering 244 upvotes, 116 comments
  16. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 160 upvotes, 74 comments
  17. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 341 upvotes, 177 comments
  18. When the chain becomes the product: Seven years inside a token-funded venture 39 upvotes, 15 comments
  19. Microsoft BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 145 upvotes, 80 comments
  20. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 230 upvotes, 142 comments
  21. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 546 upvotes, 378 comments
  22. Lego's 0.002 mm Specification and Its Implications for Manufacturing (2025) 167 upvotes, 104 comments
  23. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 357 upvotes, 271 comments
  24. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 545 upvotes, 449 comments
  25. UK MPs give ministers powers to restrict Internet for under 18s 61 upvotes, 44 comments
  26. RISC-V Is Sloooow 287 upvotes, 305 comments
  27. Agents that run while I sleep 380 upvotes, 429 comments
  28. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 198 upvotes, 238 comments
  29. Let yourself fall down more 7 upvotes, 4 comments
  30. Hurricane Electric (HE.NET) IPv6 tunnelbroker page offline due to expired domain 3 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 11:30pm, 11 March 2026. Source here.
